title: Conduit connector registry index (schemaVersion 1)
description: Top-level envelope for the signed, freeze-protected connector registry index. See docs/design-documents/20260713-connector-registry-mvp.md (Decisions 1-2) and r1-spec.md for the trust model this schema encodes. IMPORTANT: this schema validates the on-disk/served JSON shape (for index-CI linting and fixture testing). It does NOT describe verification order — see r1-spec.md §(a) for the required 'verify canonical bytes, then parse' sequence a client must follow; that sequence cannot be expressed in JSON Schema alone.

signatures.description: Detached signature(s) over the JCS-canonicalized bytes of `payload` (see r1-spec.md §(a)). Array shape is forward-compatible with threshold/multi-key signing (the named v2 escalation to full TUF); MVP populates exactly one entry, signed by the single registry root key.

payload.description: The signed payload. Every field a client trusts (urls, sha256, expectedIdentity*, versions, revocations, the monotonic counter, the timestamp) MUST live in here, not in the outer envelope -- anything outside `payload` is unauthenticated and must never be trusted for a security decision (see r1-spec.md §(a) for why schemaVersion itself is inside payload, not a sibling of it).

schemaVersion.description: Payload schema version. A client refuses (ERR_SCHEMA_TOO_NEW, ‘upgrade Conduit’) any schemaVersion greater than the highest it was built to understand. New fields must be additive within a schemaVersion; a breaking change bumps this integer and follows announce -> warn -> remove (minimum two minor Conduit versions), per repo convention.

index.description: Freeze/rollback-protection metadata (TUF-lite §2). Both fields are inside the signed payload so a compromised origin/CDN cannot forge freshness independent of the root key.

version.description: Monotonically increasing counter, incremented on every signed rebuild of the index (content change OR heartbeat re-sign -- see r1-spec.md §(b) and OPEN QUESTIONS). A client refuses (ERR_INDEX_STALE) any fetched index whose version is lower than the highest it has previously observed and verified.

timestamp.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp of this signed build. A client refuses (ERR_INDEX_STALE, distinct from ERR_INDEX_UNREACHABLE) an index whose timestamp is older than now-minus-max-staleness. Max-staleness value is an open question (see r1-spec.md).

connectors.description: One entry per registered connector name. Names are unique within the index (index-CI enforces this; JSON Schema alone cannot express cross-array uniqueness on a subfield, see r1-spec.md).

role.description: The signing authority. `root` authorizes content (connectors/publishers/versions/revocations) and is reviewer-gated. `freshness` is a separate, narrowly-scoped key a scheduled job uses unattended: a client accepts a freshness-only signature ONLY when the payload's `connectors[]` subtree is byte-identical to what it last verified under a `root` signature (i.e. it may only extend `index.timestamp`/bump `index.version`), else it requires a `root` signature. Separating these closes the root-key-double-duty hole (see r1-spec.md §(a)/OQ3 resolution): the root key is never used unattended nightly.

keyId.description: Stable identifier for the public key used, e.g. a SHA-256 fingerprint of the SPKI-encoded public key ("sha256:<hex>"). Trust anchors (root and freshness public keys) are compiled into the conduit binary at release and fixed at build time -- never updated at runtime (see r1-spec.md OQ1/OQ2 resolution).

algorithm.description: Signature algorithm. ed25519 is RECOMMENDED (small keys/signatures, no parameter-choice footguns). ecdsa-p256-sha256 listed as an HSM-compatibility fallback. Final choice is an open question (see r1-spec.md).

signature.description: Signature bytes (base64-standard), computed over the JCS-canonicalized (RFC 8785) UTF-8 bytes of the sibling `payload` object.

name.description: Unique registry name, e.g. "postgres". Resolution by `install` is exact-match only (no fuzzy/nearest-match) -- see Decision 3.

displayName.description: Human-friendly name for the web UI. Not used in any trust decision.

description.description: Short description for the web UI / `list`. Not used in any trust decision.

repository.description: Source repository URL, for the web UI and human review context. Not itself a trust input -- the trust input is `publisher.expectedIdentityPattern`, which independently encodes (and pins) the repo + workflow path.

versions.description: Published versions for this connector, newest-first by convention (not schema-enforced). Entries are append-only once published: index-CI must reject a PR that mutates any field of an already-present version other than `deprecated`, `yanked`, or `yanked.reason`/`yanked.yankedAt` (see r1-spec.md §(d)).

publisher.description: Per-name identity pinning (Decision 1) -- the actual root-of-trust decision for this connector name. Changing expectedOIDCIssuer or expectedIdentityPattern for an already-registered name requires the same human-reviewed path as first registration (see r1-spec.md §(d)).

expectedOIDCIssuer.description: OIDC issuer URL a valid signing identity must present, e.g. "https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com" for GitHub Actions keyless signing. Maps to `cosign verify --certificate-oidc-issuer`.

expectedIdentityPattern.description: MUST be a fully-anchored (^...$) regular expression matching the certificate SAN / workflow identity (repo + workflow path + ref/tag pattern), e.g. "^https://github\\.com/ConduitIO/conduit-connector-postgres/\\.github/workflows/publish\\.yml@refs/tags/v[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+$". Maps to `cosign verify --certificate-identity-regexp`. Anchoring is enforced here at the schema level (pattern requires a leading ^ and trailing $) specifically to close a partial-match impersonation bypass -- see r1-spec.md §(c) and OPEN QUESTIONS.

revoked.description: If present, this publisher identity is revoked: ALL versions under this connector name are considered compromised regardless of individual `yanked` status. `conduit connectors audit` flags every installed version of this name. Set only via the human-reviewed registration path (§1); never auto-mergeable (see r1-spec.md OPEN QUESTIONS on the yank/revoke authorization path).

reason.description: Human-readable reason, surfaced verbatim by `install` (on refusal) and `conduit connectors audit` (on flag).

revokedAt.description: When the revocation was recorded. Additive/optional, for audit/UI ordering.

revokedBy.description: Optional chain-of-custody: the reviewer/identity that recorded this revocation, for the audit trail.

reason.description: Human-readable yank reason, surfaced verbatim by `install` (on refusal) and `conduit connectors audit` (on flag).

yankedAt.description: When the version was yanked. Additive/optional field.

yankedBy.description: Optional chain-of-custody: the reviewer/identity that recorded this yank, for the audit trail.

version.description: Semver version string of this connector release, e.g. "0.14.0". Immutable once published (append-only; see `connectors[].versions` description).

releasedAt.description: When this version was published to the index. Used for web UI ordering and audit context; not itself a trust input.

minConduitVersion.description: Minimum Conduit engine version this connector version requires. `install` refuses (naming required vs. running) an incompatible selection; resolution without an explicit `@version` always prefers a compatible version.

minProtocolVersion.description: Minimum conduit-connector-protocol version this connector version requires.

artifacts.description: One entry per (os, arch) build for this version. `signature` is per-artifact (not per-version) because a cosign blob signature is over one specific artifact's digest -- see r1-spec.md OPEN QUESTIONS for the divergence from the design doc's literal field grouping and why.

slsaProvenance.description: OPTIONAL single multi-subject SLSA provenance attestation covering every artifact in this version (the common goreleaser + slsa-github-generator shape: one provenance file, multiple `subject` entries, one per artifact digest). If the eventual publish Action instead emits one attestation per artifact, this moves inside each artifact entry instead (see artifact.slsaProvenance and r1-spec.md OPEN QUESTIONS) -- both are declared in this schema and a client/index-CI MUST accept either shape until the maintainer picks one.

deprecated.description: Soft signal: still installable, but flagged (e.g. superseded, unmaintained). No structured reason field per the design doc's terse `deprecated?`; use `description`/release notes in the connector repo for detail.

yanked.description: If present, this specific version is yanked (e.g. bad build, discovered vulnerability, accidental publish). `install` refuses selecting it (naming the reason); `conduit connectors audit` flags it if already installed. Does not affect sibling versions of the same connector.

os.description: GOOS value. Enum reflects Conduit's actual supported build targets, not an invented registry-specific taxonomy.

kind.description: Artifact kind. `standalone` is the only kind installable in the MVP; `wasm` is reserved so the v0.19 WASM-connector distribution work (go/no-go item) slots in without a breaking index change. `install` must refuse an unrecognized-but-schema-valid future kind gracefully, not crash.

url.description: Scarf-gatewayed download URL. This value is INSIDE the signed payload (so a compromised index can't redirect it), but the URL is not by itself sufficient for trust: the digest is always recomputed from the actual received bytes after following redirects, so a compromised/misbehaving gateway can only cause a failed verification (denial of service), never a successful wrong-artifact install (see Decision 3, Failure modes).

sha256.description: Expected SHA-256 of the artifact bytes. This is CORRUPTION detection (integrity against a bad download), not the trust boundary -- the trust boundary is `signature` verified against the connector's pinned identity. Once index-signing (this document) is verified, this field becomes trustworthy too, and the sha256 check and the signature check converge on the same fact by design (belt-and-suspenders).

signature.description: Reference to the cosign signature bundle covering THIS artifact's own digest. Required -- an artifact with no signature reference cannot appear in a normally-published version (an actually-unsigned artifact is handled entirely client-side via `--allow-unsigned`, never by omitting this field in the index).

slsaProvenance.description: OPTIONAL per-artifact SLSA provenance, used only if the publish Action emits one attestation per artifact rather than one per version. Mutually preferred over the version-level `slsaProvenance` when both could apply; see r1-spec.md OPEN QUESTIONS.

bundleURL.description: URL to the Sigstore bundle (cosign `.sigstore`/`.bundle` JSON) for this artifact -- certificate chain + signature + Rekor inclusion proof/SET, sufficient for OFFLINE verification (no live Fulcio/Rekor query required at install time). Maps to `cosign verify --bundle <fetched-file> --certificate-identity-regexp ... --certificate-oidc-issuer ...`.

rekorLogIndex.description: OPTIONAL Rekor transparency-log index, for human cross-reference (e.g. `rekor-cli get --log-index`) and audit tooling. Not required for verification if the bundle embeds the inclusion proof.

bundleURL.description: URL to the SLSA provenance attestation bundle (in-toto statement + Sigstore bundle), verifiable offline the same way as `signatureRef.bundleURL`. Maps to `cosign verify-attestation --bundle <fetched-file> --type <predicateType> --certificate-identity-regexp ... --certificate-oidc-issuer ...`.

predicateType.description: in-toto predicate type, e.g. "https://slsa.dev/provenance/v1". A client must reject a predicate type it does not recognize rather than skip the check.
